story.iWant: a Docker Compose stack with PostgreSQL, Keycloak, Redis, Prometheus, and Grafana
story.soThat: I have all infrastructure services for local development

tasks.task: Configure health checks for all services
Commit: "Add Docker Compose development stack with all services"

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: docker-compose.yml created with services: postgres (16.10), keycloak (26.4.2), redis (7.2), prometheus, grafana (12.2)
Custom Keycloak configuration in docker/keycloak/ with realm-export.json (test users, roles)
PostgreSQL init scripts in docker/postgres/init-scripts/ (schema creation, RLS setup)
All services start successfully with docker-compose up
Health checks pass for all services
Configurable ports via environment variables (e.g., GRAFANA_PORT)

doc.title: EAF Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Specifies infrastructure requirements including PostgreSQL 16.10 with event store optimizations, Keycloak 26.4.2 for OIDC authentication, Redis 7.2 for JWT revocation cache, and observability stack (Prometheus, Grafana) for metrics and monitoring.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Requirements for one-command development environment initialization with Docker Compose managing all infrastructure dependencies. Target: <5 minute setup on clean system.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Details container configuration for PostgreSQL (event store + projections), Keycloak (realm setup, test users), Redis (revocation cache), and observability services with health check requirements.

doc.title: Story 1.4: Create Version Catalog
doc.snippet: Confirmed versions: PostgreSQL 42.7.8 (driver), Keycloak 26.4.2, Redis client 3.5.6. All versions verified against architecture.md specifications on 2025-10-30/31.

doc.title: Story 1.6: One-Command Initialization Script
doc.snippet: Depends on this story. Will create scripts/init-dev.sh that starts Docker Compose, validates health checks, installs Git hooks, and seeds test data. Target: <5 min complete initialization.

artifact.reason: Empty directory exists (.gitkeep only). This story will populate with subdirectories: postgres/, keycloak/, prometheus/ containing service configurations.

Contains verified versions for PostgreSQL (42.7.8 driver, 16.10 container), Keycloak (26.4.2), Redis (3.5.6 client, 7.2 container), Prometheus/Grafana dependencies. Use these exact versions in docker-compose.yml.

constraints.constraint: MUST use exact versions from gradle/libs.versions.toml to ensure consistency with application dependencies
PostgreSQL MUST include init scripts for: uuid-ossp extension, eaf schema, basic table structure preparation for Flyway migrations
Keycloak realm-export.json MUST include: realm 'eaf', client 'eaf-api' (confidential), test users (admin with WIDGET_ADMIN, viewer with WIDGET_VIEWER), proper tenant_id claim configuration
All services MUST have health checks configured with appropriate intervals and retries
Ports MUST be configurable via environment variables with sensible defaults (PostgreSQL:5432, Keycloak:8080, Redis:6379, Prometheus:9090, Grafana:3000)
Docker Compose MUST support both amd64 and arm64 architectures (ppc64le deferred to Epic 3 Story 3.11)
Service startup order: PostgreSQL → Redis → Keycloak → Prometheus → Grafana (use depends_on with health checks)
PostgreSQL init scripts MUST be idempotent (safe to run multiple times)

interface.name: PostgreSQL Connection
interface.signature: Host: localhost Port: 5432 (configurable via POSTGRES_PORT) Database: eaf User: eaf_user Password: eaf_password (development only) Connection String: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/eaf

interface.name: Keycloak Admin Console
interface.signature: URL: http://localhost:8080 Admin User: admin Admin Password: admin (development only) Realm: eaf Client ID: eaf-api

interface.name: Redis Cache
interface.signature: Host: localhost Port: 6379 (configurable via REDIS_PORT) Protocol: redis:// Use Case: JWT revocation blacklist (Epic 3)

interface.name: Prometheus Metrics
interface.signature: URL: http://localhost:9090 Scrape Endpoint: http://host.docker.internal:8081/actuator/prometheus Scrape Interval: 15s

interface.name: Grafana Dashboards
interface.signature: URL: http://localhost:3000 Admin User: admin Admin Password: admin (development only) Datasource: Prometheus (http://prometheus:9090)

tests.standards: Testing for infrastructure services focuses on health checks and connectivity validation. Use Testcontainers for integration tests in subsequent stories (Epic 2+). For this story, manual verification via docker-compose ps (health status) and direct connectivity tests (psql, redis-cli, curl) are sufficient. All services must reach 'healthy' status within 60 seconds of startup.

locations.location: Manual verification commands in Test Evidence section

ideas.idea: Verify docker-compose.yml syntax with `docker-compose config` (validation without starting services)
Validate realm-export.json against Keycloak schema (JSON structure check)
Test PostgreSQL init script independently: `psql -h localhost -U eaf_user -d eaf -f docker/postgres/init-scripts/01-init.sql` (idempotency check)
Validate prometheus.yml syntax with `promtool check config docker/prometheus/prometheus.yml`
Health check test: `docker-compose up -d && sleep 60 && docker-compose ps` - all services should show 'healthy' or 'Up'

Individual service health validation: PostgreSQL (psql connection), Keycloak (curl http://localhost:8080/realms/eaf), Redis (redis-cli ping), Prometheus (curl http://localhost:9090/-/ready), Grafana (curl http://localhost:3000/api/health)
Environment variable test: Set GRAFANA_PORT=3001 in .env, verify Grafana accessible at http://localhost:3001 after docker-compose up
